steps.title: Spot the comma-offset pattern
steps.content: The commas framing the blank — one after “and,” and one after “roughly one million people” — mark an offset descriptive phrase. This structure requires a present participle, not a finite verb.

steps.title: Match the participle form
steps.content: ”Containing” as a present participle correctly fits the offset position describing Yerevan. Options B and D introduce finite verbs that violate this structure; option A's past participle creates passive meaning. Only a present participle works in this offset modifier pattern — that matches choice C — C is correct.
